file_name: Columbia Laboratories, (Bermuda) Ltd. - AMEND NO. 2 TO MANUFACTURING AND SUPPLY AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Fleet shall provide such assistance as Columbia may reasonably request to ensure the orderly transfer of the manufacture of the Product to any alternative manufacturer. If requested by Columbia, Fleet shall transfer to Columbia or the alternative manufacturer all technology and know-how necessary or useful to give Columbia or the alternative manufacturer the capability of manufacturing the Product. Fleet shall communicate such technology to Columbia or the alternative manufacturer promptly, effectively and economically, so that Columbia or the alternative manufacturer can undertake the manufacture of the Product and continue the sale of the Product without interruption. Columbia undertakes to reimburse Fleet for its reasonable costs of providing such assistance and to pay to Fleet an amount for all inventory of Raw Materials and work in progress of Products and part completed Products used to provide such assistance.
perturbation.changed_text: Fleet shall provide such assistance as Columbia may reasonably request to ensure the orderly transfer of the manufacture of the Product to any alternative manufacturer. If requested by Columbia, Fleet *may* transfer to Columbia or the alternative manufacturer *some* technology and know-how *deemed appropriate* to give Columbia or the alternative manufacturer the capability of manufacturing the Product. Fleet shall communicate such technology to Columbia or the alternative manufacturer *as agreed upon*, so that Columbia or the alternative manufacturer can undertake the manufacture of the Product and continue the sale of the Product without interruption. Columbia *is not obligated* to reimburse Fleet for its reasonable costs of providing such assistance and *may choose to negotiate* an amount for all inventory of Raw Materials and work in progress of Products and part completed Products used to provide such assistance.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Fleet *shall* transfer all technology and know-how, and Columbia *undertakes* to reimburse Fleet. The modified version changes *shall* to *may*, *all* to *some*, adds the qualifier *deemed appropriate*, *as agreed upon* and replaces 'undertakes' with *is not obligated*, creating uncertainty and conflicting obligations. This directly contradicts the intention of a smooth technology transfer, enabling disputes over what constitutes 'appropriate' technology and who bears the costs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fleet shall provide such assistance as Columbia may reasonably request to ensure the orderly transfer of the manufacture of the Product to any alternative manufacturer. If requested by Columbia, Fleet *may* transfer to Columbia or the alternative manufacturer *some* technology and know-how *deemed appropriate* to give Columbia or the alternative manufacturer the capability of manufacturing the Product. Fleet shall communicate such technology to Columbia or the alternative manufacturer *as agreed upon*, so that Columbia or the alternative manufacturer can undertake the manufacture of the Product and continue the sale of the Product without interruption. Columbia *is not obligated* to reimburse Fleet for its reasonable costs of providing such assistance and *may choose to negotiate* an amount for all inventory of Raw Materials and work in progress of Products and part completed Products used to provide such assistance.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a strong contradiction by changing mandatory obligations ('shall,' 'undertakes') into discretionary actions ('may,' 'is not obligated'). Specifically, the original text mandates Fleet to transfer all technology and know-how and Columbia to reimburse Fleet's costs. The changed text makes the technology transfer discretionary ('may transfer some technology deemed appropriate') and removes the obligation for Columbia to reimburse Fleet ('is not obligated to reimburse'). This creates a direct conflict regarding the responsibilities of both parties in the technology transfer process, leading to potential disputes and rendering the agreement ambiguous and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Fleet shall maintain the Columbia Equipment, the reasonable costs of which shall be agreed by the Parties (acting reasonably) and paid by Columbia, and: (a) maintenance shall be carried out to at least the standards adopted in respect of Fleet's other machinery and equipment used by it at its premises and Fleet shall not prioritize the maintenance of its own equipment above that of the Columbia Equipment; (b) Fleet shall at all times ensure that it has sufficient trained and competent maintenance personnel available for such maintenance; (c) during the Term, on or before 1 January of each year, Fleet shall prepare and deliver to Columbia: (i) a maintenance plan setting out the maintenance activities to be performed by Fleet in respect of the Columbia Equipment for the following year; and
perturbation.changed_text: Fleet shall maintain the Columbia Equipment, the reasonable costs of which *may* be agreed by the Parties (acting reasonably) and *may* be paid by Columbia, and: (a) maintenance *should attempt* to be carried out to at least the standards adopted in respect of Fleet's other machinery and equipment used by it at its premises and Fleet shall not prioritize the maintenance of its own equipment above that of the Columbia Equipment; (b) Fleet shall at all times ensure that it has sufficient trained and competent maintenance personnel available for such maintenance; (c) during the Term, on or before 1 January of each year, Fleet shall prepare and deliver to Columbia: (i) a maintenance plan setting out the maintenance activities to be performed by Fleet in respect of the Columbia Equipment for the following year; and
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation replaces *shall* with *may* regarding cost agreement and payment, and introduces 'should attempt' to introduce ambiguity on the standard of maintenance, whereas it was formally set as 'at least the standard adopted'. This introduces a contradiction: Fleet is obligated to create a maintenance plan (Section 3A.5(c)), but Columbia's payment for said maintainance is subject to a discretionary agreement (using the word 'may'). This contradiction makes the enforcement of maintenance standards uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fleet shall maintain the Columbia Equipment, the reasonable costs of which *may* be agreed by the Parties (acting reasonably) and *may* be paid by Columbia, and: (a) maintenance *should attempt* to be carried out to at least the standards adopted in respect of Fleet's other machinery and equipment used by it at its premises and Fleet shall not prioritize the maintenance of its own equipment above that of the Columbia Equipment; (b) Fleet shall at all times ensure that it has sufficient trained and competent maintenance personnel available for such maintenance; (c) during the Term, on or before 1 January of each year, Fleet shall prepare and deliver to Columbia: (i) a maintenance plan setting out the maintenance activities to be performed by Fleet in respect of the Columbia Equipment for the following year; and
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligations of Fleet. The original text stated 'Fleet shall maintain...costs of which shall be agreed by the Parties...and paid by Columbia.' The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may,' making the cost agreement and payment discretionary. It also changes 'maintenance shall be carried out' to 'maintenance should attempt to be carried out', creating ambiguity about the standard of maintenance. This directly contradicts the original intent of ensuring consistent maintenance and creates uncertainty about the enforcement of maintenance standards, as Columbia might not agree to pay for the maintenance activities outlined in the plan required by Section 3A.5(c). Because 'may' payment contradicts the creation of a maintenance plan, there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Fleet shall provide Columbia with access to all books and records necessary to verify any changes to the purchase price and any underpayment or overpayment.
perturbation.changed_text: Fleet *may* provide Columbia with access to *some* books and records *deemed necessary by Fleet* to verify any changes to the purchase price and any underpayment or overpayment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates Fleet to provide Columbia with access to *all* necessary books and records for verification, whereas the change uses *may* and introduces the element that Fleet decides what access is *deemed necessary by Fleet*, giving Fleet discretion, and conflicting with the initial commitment. The modified sentence will contradict the auditing possibilities mentioned in *Section 4.1 Audits* that mentions that Columbia QA can audit Fleet's manifacturing facility
perturbation.contradicted_text: "4.1 Audits. Columbia QA, any other person appointed by Columbia, Columbia's customer, and/or any Regulatory Authority may conduct inspections and audits of Fleet's manufacturing facility, Columbia Equipment, quality control laboratories, and other quality systems relating to the manufacture and storage of the Product according to Columbia's reasonable procedures upon reasonable prior written notice, during normal business hours, provided, however, that Columbia QA, any other person appointed by Columbia and/or any Regulatory Authority may conduct a "For Cause" audit during normal business hours upon three (3) business days prior written notice to Fleet. Any such audit undertaken by Columbia QA or any other person appointed by Columbia shall be at Columbia's sole cost and expense. Columbia or any other person appointed by Columbia shall have the right, in connection with any such audit, to inspect and obtain copies of any records or other documents and materials associated with or related to the manufacture of the Product. Fleet shall promptly notify Columbia of any proposed inspections by any governmental authority of the facilities at which Product is manufactured in sufficient time for Columbia to attend such inspection."
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Fleet to provide Columbia with access to all necessary books and records, while the changed text allows Fleet to selectively provide access to records deemed necessary by Fleet. This contradicts Section 4.1, which grants Columbia QA and other authorized parties the right to inspect and obtain copies of *any* records related to the manufacture of the Product. The change introduces an element of Fleet's discretion, directly conflicting with Columbia's unrestricted access rights as stipulated in Section 4.1.
